kill_fact: No kill_fact. The bear case survives independent primary-source attack: every load-bearing bear fact verifies against SEC filings / company releases. Q1-2026 10-Q confirms revenue is $43.4M of DOE ARDP grant/cost-share income (NOT product sales), opex +133% YoY to $109.5M, operating burn $67.3M/qtr, net loss $166.2M including a $108.9M non-cash warrant mark, and total liquidity $944.0M at 3/31 + ~$1.1B IPO net = ~$2.0B with zero debt. First commercial power is early-2030s; the '11.5 GWe / 144-reactor' figure is explicitly contingent ('assuming each customer exercises its contingent rights in full') = optionality, not contracted orders. At ~$8.74B market cap on grant income for a 7-9yr terminal option sharing the single AI-capex driver with the rest of Basket E, the bear's 'priced as a high-probability winner, not the venture option it is' thesis holds. No primary fact breaks the bear (REFUTED) case.
corrections: (1) LOCKUP DATE: debate says '~October 2026'; the lockup was ACCELERATED to September 1, 2026 (ten trading days before the quarterly blackout), per the S-1/A lock-up agreement language — the mechanical supply / washout risk arrives ~6 weeks EARLIER than the dossier states, strengthening the bear's near-term-overhang point. (2) DOW SEADRIFT CPA MISLABELED: the dossier and forcing_function treat the Dow construction-permit application as a future event 'expected Q1-2027.' It is NOT pending-to-file — the CPA was FILED March 31, 2025, and the NRC already issued an Environmental Assessment / Finding of No Significant Impact (FONSI) in May 2026. The real Q1-2027 checkpoint is CPA ISSUANCE, not filing; the bull's 'construction permit expected Q1-2027' framing overstates how soft this catalyst is (it is materially de-risked vs the dossier's depiction), but it does NOT change the early-2030s first-power timeline that anchors the bear. (3) AMAZON CPA 'SLIP Q4-26 to H1-27': this specific load-bearing bear claim could NOT be independently confirmed against a primary source — Cascade/Energy Northwest is still in NRC PRE-APPLICATION; no Cascade CPA has been filed, and the Q1-2026 release does not state a CPA date or a slip. Treat the 'slipped two quarters' detail as UNVERIFIED transcript color, not a confirmed fact. (4) NRC license nuance: it is the 'first NEW fuel FACILITIES licensed in over 50 years' (Part-70 Special Nuclear Material license, granted Feb 13 2026, initial 40-yr term, TX-1 = first US Category II fuel facility) — accurate, but the precise wording is 'facilities,' not 'commercial fuel-fabrication license.'
evidence: SEC Form 10-Q, X-Energy Inc, period ended 2026-03-31 (filed 2026-06): total liquidity $944.0M (cash $224.1M + ST investments $449.5M + LT $270.4M), zero debt; revenue/grant income $43.4M (+109%, ~$39.9M DOE ARDP cost-share); opex $109.5M (+133%); net loss $166.2M incl. $108.9M warrant mark; operating cash burn $67.3M; ~$1.1B IPO net proceeds subsequent to quarter-end.

SEC Form 424B4 / 8-K (2026-04): IPO priced 2026-04-23 at $23.00/share, traded on Nasdaq from 2026-04-24, ~$1.0-1.1B raised; X-Energy IR 'Reports First Quarter 2026 Results' (globenewswire, 2026-06-04): pipeline = '144 reactors... approximately 11.5 gigawatts electric, assuming each customer exercises its contingent rights in full' (contingent, not contracted); Dow Seadrift NRC FONSI issued May 2026.

S-1/A lock-up agreement (sec.gov, 2026): 180-day restricted period from 2026-04-23 originally ending 2026-10-20, ACCELERATED to 2026-09-01 (ten trading days before the quarterly blackout) — earlier supply overhang than the '~October 2026' dossier figure.

x-energy.com / energy.gov (2026-02-13): TRISO-X awarded first-ever NRC Part-70 HALEU fuel-fabrication license (initial 40-yr term; TX-1/TX-2 = first new US fuel facilities licensed in >50 years, TX-1 first US Category II fuel facility); POWER Magazine: Dow + X-energy Seadrift construction-permit application FILED 2025-03-31 (NRC review up to ~30 months).
